qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Dynamic node parameter routing lets a read-only credential sharee exfiltrate authenticated secrets
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the dynamic node-parameters options endpoint and RoutingNode credential application path, while the advisory targets sandbox escape from the Python Code node task runner path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding is a credential exfiltration issue in `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options`; it does not identify the advisory's Python Code node sandbox escape or the Python task runner vulnerable path.
evidence: The advisory scopes the issue to an authenticated user who can create or modify workflows containing a Python Code node and escape the Python Task Runner sandbox to achieve code execution on the task runner container.

The qualifying finding's exploit path is `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options -> getOptionsViaLoadOptions() -> RoutingNode.runNode() -> httpRequestWithAuthentication()`, with reported code locations in `packages/cli/src/controllers/dynamic-node-parameters.controller.ts`, `packages/cli/src/services/dynamic-node-parameters.service.ts`, `packages/core/src/execution-engine/routing-node.ts`, and request-helper code.

The repository's Python Code node path is implemented through `packages/nodes-base/nodes/Code/PythonTaskRunnerSandbox.ts`, which forwards Python code to `startJob('python', ...)`, with the runner implemented under `packages/@n8n/task-runner-python`; the qualifying finding does not describe or exercise this component.
